mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in low-unemployment regimes fall below 0.5% in 40%+ of cases despite sustained UNRATE declines.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day surge in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 120-day moving average signals strengthening manufacturing activity that drives equity risk premiums higher, producing positive SPX returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in INDPRO expansion phases show negative correlation (Pearson < -0.15) with industrial output growth over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in both GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signal deteriorating private sector demand, triggering defensive equity rotation that produces negative SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in co-contraction scenarios exceed 1.2% in 30%+ of cases despite simultaneous GDPC1/HOUST declines.

mechanism_narrative: A 10-day breakout above 2.5% in the 3-month change of CPIAUCSL signals inflation persistence that forces central bank rate hikes, generating negative SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 50%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL 3m chg exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below its 120-day average signals weakening labor demand that depresses consumer spending, producing negative SPX returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in PAYEMS contraction phases show positive correlation (Pearson > 0.2) with employment declines over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in both consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP (GDPC1) above their respective 120-day averages signals robust demand dynamics that drive SPX positive returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in co-expansion scenarios fall below 0.8% in 40%+ of cases despite simultaneous RSAFS/GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 60-day moving average signals manufacturing weakness that triggers equity sell-offs, producing negative SPX returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in INDPRO contraction phases exceed 1.0% in 35%+ of cases despite manufacturing declines.
